name: Chief's Luau
tagline: West Side luau at Wet 'n' Wild with solid fire knife dancing, a big buffet, and Waikiki pickup — the approachable mid-range option.
hours: Gates open ~5pm, show ~6–8:30pm. Days vary, check website
bestTime: Book a few days ahead for weekend shows. The Waikiki shuttle makes logistics easy
parking: Free parking at Wet 'n' Wild Hawaii, 400 Farrington Hwy
talkStory: Chief's Luau runs out of the Wet 'n' Wild Hawaii waterpark in Kapolei, which sounds odd but actually works — the outdoor amphitheater space is solid and the Leeward sunset backdrop is beautiful. The fire knife dance is the highlight, with Chief Sielu (a world fire knife champion) headlining the show. The buffet is standard luau fare — kalua pig, chicken long rice, poi, haupia — done competently. It's bigger and more polished than a backyard luau but more affordable and less corporate than the Waikiki hotel luaus. Hotel pickup from Waikiki is available and recommended since it's a 30+ minute drive.
mustOrder: upgrade to the Royal package for better seating
